A S S A M ACT XIII OF 1943 An Act to provide for and regulate the holidays, payment of wages and leave of persons employed in shops, commercial establishments and establishments for public entertainment or amusement, and the hours of work of persons so employed. Preamble.—Whereas it is expedient to provide for and regulate the holidays, pavment of wages and leave of persons employed in shops, commercial establish- ments and establishments for public entertainment or amusement, and the hours of work of persons so employed; It is hereby enacted as follows : — t Short title, commencement and application.—(1) This Act may be called 8ta Assam Shops and Establishments Act. 1948. « (2) It shall come into force on such date as the Chief Commissioner -relax. b ~ notification, appoint. fSS i t shall arjply in the first instance to the Municipality of Imphal as declaim' or constituted u n d £ the provisions of the Assam Municipal Act. 1956 (Assam Ac 15 of 1957) as in force in the Union territory of Manipur an<? thereafter iS * a U ™olv to such other areas, or to such shops, commercial establishments or establish- ments for the public entertainment or amusement in such other areas m the Chief Commissioner may, by notification, specify. 2. Definitions.—In this Act, unless there is anything repugnant in fl* subject or context— (1) "Chief Commissioner" means the Chief Commissioner of Manipur; flA) "Child" means a person who has not completed his twelfth year of age; (2) "Closed" means not open for the service of any customer; m "commercial establishment" means an establishment in which there is 0 9 ronducte: the business of advertising, commission forwarding or commercial agency, a department of a factory in which persons are empSyed in a clerical capacity in any room or place w k . r e no S u f acturing process is being carried on, a f t o a l department of any industrial or commercial undertaking including one a ^ « » ™ * ; rfort an insurance company, joint stock company, b r o k e r s office, cr - x r h a n e e or such other establishment or class thereof as the d u e l r S i ^ r n l n y 1)7 notification, declare to be a commercial e ^ r S m e r S for he purposes of this Act, but does not include a I S l o r ™n establishment for public entertainment or amusement; <r» "day" meaas a period of twenty four hours beginning at midnight: ™, „.=1Tin1nirra7,:, tass-B a person owning or having charge of the business m " g ^ W j B ^ J ^ e J g S j S or establishment for public entertain- - ,'i -- 2 of 8 -- 3 ment or amusement, and includes an agent or m a n a g V - o i r ^ a n d any other person acting on behalf of, such person in the general manage- ment or control of such establishment; (6) 'establishment for public entertainment or amusement" means a restaurant, eating-house, cafe, cinema, theatre and such other establishment or class thereof as the Chief Commissioner may. by notification, declare to be, for the purposes of this Act, an establish- ment for public entertainment or amusement, but does not include .a shop or a commercial establishment; (7) "factory" means a factory as defined in, or declared to be a factory under the Factories Act, 1948 (Act 63 of 1948); (8) "Half, day" means a period of six consecutive hours between the hours of half past seven o'clock ante meridiem and seven o clock post meridiem; O) "notification" means a notification published in the Manipur Gazette; (10) "person employed" means— (i) in the case of a. shop, a person wholly or principally employed in the shop in connection with the business of the shop, tti) in the case of a commercial establishment, a person wholly or principally employed in connection with the business of the estab- lishment or in the case of a factory, a person employed in a clerical capacity in any room oer place where no manufacturing process is being carried on, (iii) in the case of an establishment for public entertainment or amuse- ment a person wholly or principally employed in the preparation or servine of food, or drink, or in attendance upon customers, or in cleaning any part of the establishment or the utensils used therein or as attendant, cashier, clerk, doorkeeper, operator or usher or in some other similar capacity but does not include a shop-keeper or employer or the husband, wife, child, father, mother, brother or sister of a shop-keeper, or employer who lives with, and is dependent on, such shop-keeper or employer; (11) "prescribed" means prescribed by rules; (12) "rules" means rules made under this Act; (13) "shop': means any premises used wholly or in p a r i for the wholesale or retail sale of commodities or articles, either for uash or m credit, and such other premises as the Chief Commissioner may. by notification declare to be a shop for the purposes of this Act, but does no include a commercial establishment or an establishment for public entertain- ment or amusement; (14) "shop-keeper" means a person owninp or having charge of the busi- ness of a shoo, and includes an agent or manager, of, and any othei person a c t i n g on behalf of, such person in the general m a n a g e m e n t or control of a shop; (15) "week" m e a n s a period of seven days beginning at midnight on S a t u r d a y ; and (16) "year" means the period beginning on J a n u a r y the first, and ending on December 3lst. 3 Reference to time of ilay.—References to the time of day i n this Act^ shall be toed to be references to Indian Standard -Time, . - ^ y e time ttsat the Chief Commissioner -nay prescribe as SUmdard d i m e tor the Union t e m t o r j of &Eanipur. -- 3 of 8 -- . 4. P ^ c •entertainment or a m u s e m e n t any establishment which is not a shop or a commercial establishment ft The provisions of this Act specified in a notification under sub-section (B shall apply io any premises or establishment which, under the provisions of that sub-section, has been declared to ba a shop or a commercial establishment or an establishment for public entertainment or amusement as the case m a y be. 3 The Chief Commissioner may, by notification on account of such holidav or- ot her occasion as may be prescribed, suspend the operation of all or any of the provisions of this Act in respect Of any shop or commercial establishment, or establashmeat for public e n t e r t a i n m e n t or amusement or of any class of shoos or establishments as aforesaid for such period and subject to such conditions as he chmks fit. 5. Act not ainulicajjle in certain flS'taMishiueats, shops and nersons (11 Nothing hi this Act shall apply to— (a) offices of or under the Central Government or Manipur Administration i-he Reserve Bank of India or any other bank, or any local authority; (b) any water transport service, motor service, postal, telegraph or tele- pnone service, any system of public conservancy or sanitation, any industry, business or undertaking which supplies power, light or water to the public and such other public utility companies or associa- tions or classes thereof as the Chief Commissioner may, by notifica- tion, exempt from the operation of this Act; (c) clubs, residential hotels and boarding-houses; (d) stalls and refreshment rooms at airports; " ^ (e) establishments for the t r e a t m e n t or care of the sick, infirm destitute or mentally unfit; (f) shops or classes of shops, dealing mainly hi vegetables, meat, fish, dairy produce, bread, pastries, sweetmeats, flowers so far as the retail sale ot these articles is concerned; •(g) pharmacies or shops dealing mainly in medicines, surgical appliances bandages or other medical requisites, so far as the sale of these articles is concerned; (h) shops dealing in articles required for funerals, burials or cremations so far as the sale of these articles is concerned; (i) shops dealing in tobacco, cigars, cheeroots, cigarettes, b i n s , pan liquid refreshments sold retail for consumption on the premises ice news- papers or periodicals, so far v.s the sale of these articles is concerned; (j) omitted. G Exemption of child,—No child shall be employed in any shop commercial establishment, or establishment for public entertainment or amusement 7. Closing of shops.—(1) Every shop shall be closed on one day in each week. (2) The Chief Commissioner may, by notification, require in respect of everv shop or ot any specified class of shops that they shall be closed at such hour as may be specified m the afternoon of one specified day in each week, in addition to the day referred to in sub-section (1): Provided that, when there are conducted in a shop two or more trades o'- busmesses any of which is of such a character that, if it was the sole trade or business therein conducted, the provisions of this Act would not apply to that shop, such shop shall, so far as the conduct of that trade or business is concerned be exempt from the operation of this sub-section and of sub-section (1). P a r e n t ol w a s e s . ^ 1 1 « ^ ^ f » g S S S r S ^ a t S t o S that in resoect of which such wages are payable. 13 jferor-aSff** Person employed in a shop, commercial[establishment or establishnint for public entertainment or amusement shall be e m i t l e d - t t i after every twelve months' continuous employment to privilege leave on lull[pay for a total period not exceeding sixteen days, Or, in every year, to casual leave on &ffl pay for a .total period not exceeding ten. days, and ,.-.) after every twelve months7 eosttauoua employment, to leave on medical ^ S S S t e on half pay for a perlold not exceedmg one month ,ucn -- 6 of 8 -- rt certificate to be provided by a registered practitioner as defined in clause (bi of section 2 of Assam Medical Act, 1916 (Assam Act 1 of 1016): Provided that neither privilege leave admissible under clause (a) nor casual leave admissible under clause (b) shall be accumulated. 14. Holidays .for religious purposes.—Every person employed in a shop, commer- cial establishment or establishment for public e n t e r t a i n m e n t or amusement shall be entitled to leave or absence for any days he m a y select, not exceeding three in number in any one year, for the purpose of attending ceremonies or performing functions or duties connected with on enjoined by his religion. 15. Maintenance of records, registers and notices.—(1) Every shop-keeper and employer of an establishment for public e n t e r t a i n m e n t or amusement shall for the purposes of this Act maintain such records and registers, and display such notices, as m a y be prescribed. (2) Every employer of a commercial establishment shall for the purposes of this Act maintain such records and registers as m a y be prescribed. 16. Inspection.— (1) The Chief Commissioner may, by notification, appoint such persons or such class of persons as he thinks fit to be Inspectors for the purposes of this Act within such local limits as he may assign to them respectively. (2) All Inspectors appointed under sub-section (1) shall be deemed to b e public servants within the meaning of section 21 of the Indian Penal Code, 1860 (Act XLV of I860). 17. Power of Inspectors.—Subject as may be prescribed, an Inspector appointed under section 16 may, for the purposes of this Act and within the local limits for which he is appointed, at all reasonable times e n t e r into any place w h i c h is, or which he has reason to believe, is, a shop or a commercial establishment or an establishment for public entertainment or amusement, w i t h sucb assistants, being public servants, as may be prescribed, and m a k e such examination of t h a t place and of any record, register or notice maintained therein u n d e r the provisions of section 15 and m a y require such explanation of any such record, register or notice as he may consider necessary for the purposes of this Act: Provided that no person shall be required u n d e r this section to answer any question or give any evidence tending to criminate himself. 18. Penalties.—(I) Whoever, being an employer or shop-beeper, i jntravenes any of the provisions of sections 7, 8, 9, 10, or 11 shall, on conviction, be punish- able with fine which, for a first offence, may extend to two hundred and fifty rupees and. for a second or any subsequent offence, m a y extend to five hundred rupees. (2) Whoever, being an employer or shop-keeper, contravenes any of t h e provi- sions of sections 6, 12, 13, 14 or 15 and whoever, having custody of any record, register or notice maintained under the provisions of section 15, refuses or, w i t h - out sufficient cause, fails to produce it on being so required by a n Inspector under the provisions of section 17, shall, on conviction, be punishable with fine which may extend to fift;/ rupees. 19. Procedure.—•(!') No Court inferior to a Magistrate of the .First Class shall try an onienee punishable u n d e r this Act. (2) No Court shall take cognizance of an offence punishable under this Act except upon complaint made by an Inspector appointed u n d e r section lfl. 20. Indemnity.—No suit, prosecution or legal p r e c e d i n g w h a t e v e r shall lie against any person in respect al anything in good faith done or intended to be done under this Act or the rules. 21. Saving of certain elg&ts and privileges.—Kfothbg in this Act shall affect any rieht or orivilege to which any j<-rson employed in any shop, commercial estab- lishment "or establishment $w public entertainment or amusement is entitled at the date of the commencement of this Act u n d e r a n y other law for the time being hi force or under any contract, custom or usage which is in force on that date, ii such right or privilege is more favourable to him ttian any eight or privilege con- ferred upon him by this Act. -- 7 of 8 -- 3 . - 22 Power to make rules—(1) The Chief Commissioner may, after previous publication, make rules for carrying out the purposes of this Act.
